title: --bind flag overrides bind_addr in all modes + non-loopback startup warning
action: Add --bind <addr> to parse_args; after loading config, if present parse it as SocketAddr (typed error on bad value) and override config.bind_addr. Works with --demo and --config. When the resolved bind IP is not loopback, log a WARN + print a one-line security notice that auth is the only boundary and to prefer tailnet/TLS. Update --help.

truths: --bind 0.0.0.0:8787 makes the server reachable on all interfaces
bad --bind value is a clear error
non-loopback bind prints a security warning
prohibitions: Do not change the loopback default

trace.outcome: Added --bind <addr> overriding config.bind_addr in all modes (demo + config), typed error on bad value, WARN + security notice when binding non-loopback (auth becomes the only boundary). Verified 0.0.0.0 reachable from LAN IP. Loopback default unchanged, auth untouched.
trace.verification_evidence: 74 Rust tests + clippy -D warnings clean. Runtime-verified: herdctl --demo --bind 0.0.0.0:8788 prints the non-loopback warning and answers /api/health 200 on both 127.0.0.1 and the host LAN IP (192.168.20.243).
trace.verify_output: 74 tests + clippy clean; runtime: --demo --bind 0.0.0.0:8788 reachable via 127.0.0.1 (200) and LAN IP 192.168.20.243 (200), warning printed
